Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First I need to remember what the task is first identify the anomalies in the given POWL model then come up with hypotheses for why they exist then write PostgreSQL queries to verify them using the three tables

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The ideal flow is R A E P N C no loops So thats an anomaly the redundant loop between E and P allows repeated evaluation and approval cycles which shouldnt happen in a standard claim process

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The XOR means that either you do N or you do nothing the silent transition So the process allows skipping the customer notification step entirely But in the ideal process N is mandatory right So thats another anomaly customer notification can be omitted

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The partial order includes a direct edge from A to C and omits any edge requiring C to occur after the loop or the XOR step This means C can be executed immediately after A is completed without ever performing evaluation approval or customer notification

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets confirm the partial order edges again root order has edges R A A loop loop xor A C So Cs only precedence is that it has to come after A but it doesnt have to come after loop or xor

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: So yes you could do the loop then close the claim then do the xor which would be either N or skip So that means you can close before notifying the customer even if you did evaluate and approve

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The loop allows multiple E and P events and also allows exiting after E without ever executing P So that means you can have a claim thats evaluated but never approved or approved multiple times

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Maybe the business recently decided to allow re evaluation of claims if new information comes in hence the loop but didnt properly constrain it to only allow re evaluation under specific conditions

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Maybe the business decided to make customer notification optional for low value claims but the process modeler used a blanket XOR instead of a conditional so all claims can skip notification

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: The direct A C edge might be from a recent change allowing adjusters to fast track trivial claims by closing them immediately after assignment but the change was implemented without adding constraints

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The process modeler might have misunderstood requirements for example the business said evaluation can be repeated if needed but the modeler implemented an unrestricted loop instead of a loop with a condition

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Different departments claims customer service compliance might have given conflicting requirements and the modeler tried to accommodate all without resolving conflicts leading to the partial order that allows premature closure

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: The process modeler tool might have a bug that incorrectly adds edges when dragging and dropping activities leading to the unintended A C edge

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: The organization doesnt have process modeling standards or validation rules no rule that all mandatory steps like N cant be made optional no rule that closure C must come after all processing steps no rule that loops must have exit conditions defined

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets list the anomalies we need to detect and write a query for each claims closed without proper evaluation or approval claims with multiple evaluation or approval events claims where customer notification N is missing claims where C Close happens before E P or N

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Find claims closed without any evaluation or approval events no E or P recorded at all Those are claims closed without any evaluation or approval which is a direct result of the A C edge allowing C after A without E P

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Find claims where the close event C occurs before the first evaluation E or approval P event That catches cases where you close before evaluating or approving even if those steps happened later

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Find claims with multiple evaluation E events or multiple approval P events Because the ideal process has exactly one E and one P per claim

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Find claims that were approved have P event but have no N event and are closed So those are cases where notification was skipped which is the XOR anomaly

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Find claims where notification N occurred after claim closure C That catches cases where even if N was done it was after closing which is a problem

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Find claims that were evaluated but never approved which could be because the loop exited after E But if they are closed thats a problem

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: If this shows that auto insurance claims for example have a higher skip rate and lower average amount that supports the hypothesis that the optional notification was intended for low value claims but was applied broadly

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: If home insurance claims in certain regions have higher loop rates that might mean the loop was intended for complex home claims but if auto claims also have high rates thats unintended

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets make sure each query is tied to a specific anomaly and explain what it detects Also note assumptions like the resource field for activity A is the adjusters name or that activity labels match exactly R A E P N C

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait also for Query 3 the HAVING clause has a typo COUNT CASE WHERE should be COUNT CASE WHEN Then 1 End Oops thats a mistake need to correct that
